MAX HEALTHCARE | AGGRESSIVE EXPANSION & MEDICAL TOURISM PUSH

FY27 Capacity Plans
- Adding ~1,200 new beds in FY27 — taking total capacity to ~6,500 beds
- Long-term goal: 10,000 beds over next 3–4 years via Centres of Excellence
- Rs 6,000 crore investment announced by 2028 to add 3,700 beds across key locations
- Capex to be funded entirely through internal accruals

Recent Milestones
- Launched new 400-bed tower at Max Smart Speciality Hospital, Saket (Delhi)
- New tower houses 120+ critical care beds, 10 modular OTs, advanced Cath Labs
- Saket complex now has 1,160+ beds across 35+ specialties
- Acquired controlling stake (58.4%) in 250-bed Kalinga Hospital, Bhubaneswar — first foray into eastern India
- Opened 300-bed facility in Mumbai; 200-bed unit in Mohali; 500-bed Gurgaon facility expected in 6–7 months

Acquisition Playbook
- Eyeing 10+ acre land parcel in a city centre with existing 250-bed facility, expandable to 800–1,000 beds
- Mirrors earlier playbook in Lucknow & Mumbai — acquire, renovate, turn around

Expansion Strategy
- CMD Abhay Soi: ~20 cities identified as expansion targets due to low penetration
- Entry filter: cities where at least 2 peers already operate viably
- Target: 20–25% pre-tax ROCE within 4–5 years of investment

Medical Tourism Push
- Overseas offices now at 23 globally, up from just 2 four years ago
- Patients currently arriving from 145 countries; medical tourism growing at 25% annually
- Saket facility positioned to make Delhi-NCR a hub for medical value travel
- Fully aligned with Centre's 'Heal in India' initiative

Key Takeaway
- Max Healthcare is executing one of India's most aggressive hospital expansion blueprints
- Combination of brownfield acquisitions, greenfield builds & medical tourism strategy positions it well for the next growth decade

reports.text:
7-SEATER EV SUV SEGMENT | 5X SURGE UNDERWAY IN 2026

Market Size & Growth
- 2025 sales: Under 10,000 units of 7-seater EVs in India
- 2026 forecast: 50,000+ units — over 5x growth, fastest-growing EV segment per S&P Global Mobility
- Monthly passenger EV registrations crossed 22,000 units in March 2026, up from 5,000–6,000 two years ago
- EV penetration doubled to ~5% in just two years

Segment Price Tiers
- Entry level: Rs 20–30 lakh
- Mid segment: Rs 30–50 lakh
- Premium: Rs 50–75 lakh
- Claimed range: 400–679 km; real-world range typically 20–30% lower than certified

Key Players in the Race
- Mahindra & Mahindra — XEV 9S launched at Rs 19.95 lakh; plans to double EV capacity from April 2026
- BYD, JSW MG Motor & Kia — active in the segment
- VinFast (Vietnam) — launched VF MPV7, its third India launch in under 9 months
- VinFast India CEO Tapan Kumar Ghosh — targeting "7 adults comfortably, city + outside, comfort + range"

Demand Drivers
- Larger urban families seeking space, comfort & safety
- Low GST advantage on EVs vs ICE vehicles
- Rising disposable incomes enabling premium EV purchases
- Government push via FAME-II and state EV policies
- Puneet Gupta (S&P Global Mobility) highlights "compelling value economics" via favourable GST regime

Key Bottlenecks
- Patchy intercity fast-charging infrastructure — currently 1 public charging station per 235 EVs
- Price points still out of reach for middle-class families
- Affluent consumers with home charging access remain the primary buyers

Key Takeaway
- 7-seater EVs are the sweet spot for affluent urban Indian families in 2026
- Charging infrastructure remains the single biggest bottleneck to mass-market acceleration
- Wave of new model launches expected to widen choice and accelerate adoption further

**NOMURA ON GUJARAT GAS | BUY**

**Stance & Target**
- Recommendation: Buy
- Target Price: Rs 390

- Iran crisis may provide multiple tailwinds for Gujarat Gas — opportunity in adversity
- Recent stock correction seen as an attractive entry point for investors

- Morbi ceramic cluster outlook improves meaningfully — key volume driver

- Accounts for increased margins in the industrial segment & slightly higher volumes

**Key Takeaway**
- Near-term disruption from Iran crisis paradoxically creates long-term positives for gas distribution
- Nomura turns constructive with a Buy — sees current levels as a compelling entry opportunity

- Total buyback size: Rs 15,000 crore — largest ever by Wipro

- KFin offers faster growth; CAMS offers stability & dominance

VEDANTA | FIR FILED AFTER FATAL SAKTI PLANT BLAST

The Incident
- Blast occurred on April 14 at Vedanta's power plant in Singhitarai village, Sakti district, Chhattisgarh
- A steel tube carrying high-pressure steam from the boiler to the turbine burst, triggering the explosion
- Furnace pressure spiked suddenly within 1-2 seconds at 2:33 PM, leaving no time for shutdown
- Death toll: 20 killed; 16 injured
- Deceased workers belonged to West Bengal, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, UP, Bihar & MP

FIR Details
- FIR registered at Dabhra Police Station against Vedanta Group Chairman Anil Agarwal and others
- Plant Head Devendra Patel also named in the FIR
- Charges filed under three sections of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS)
- Sec 106 — Causing death by negligence (up to 5 years imprisonment)
- Sec 289 — Negligent conduct with respect to machinery
- Sec 3(5) — Common intention

Investigation Status
- FSL (Forensic Science Lab) report confirms excessive fuel build-up & pressure as primary cause
- Industrial Health & Safety Department's boiler inspector examined the site for 6 hours
- FIR registered on basis of boiler inspector's report
- Preliminary findings point to serious lapses in plant operations

Vedanta's Response
- Anil Agarwal expressed grief publicly, stating "your tears are mine"
- Company said it is "saddened" by the tragic incident

- Pernod India posted strong 11% sales growth in Mar'26
- United Spirits estimated to have lost marginal market share to Pernod in Mar'26

**Key Takeaway**
- Competitive pressure from Pernod India is intensifying

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 16 April 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

ELITECON: Secures ₹202 Cr export order from South Africa for cigarette & tobacco supply over 2 years.

RVNL: Wins ₹967.66 Cr railway project from East Coast Railway for rail line expansion & bridges.

REFEX INDUSTRIES: Bags ₹32.45 Cr order for pond ash transportation for road construction projects.

RISHABH INSTRUMENTS: Subsidiary Lumel secures €3M (~₹30 Cr) repeat order from German energy company.

MARINE ELECTRICALS: Secures multiple orders worth ₹16.9 Cr across infra & shipbuilding segments.

ITCONS E-SOLUTIONS: Wins ₹8.53 Cr manpower deployment contract from Indian Army for 1 year.

UNITED DRILLING TOOLS: Bags ₹5.03 Cr ONGC order for supply of drilling equipment.

MIC ELECTRONICS: Secures railway orders worth ~₹2.21 Cr from Central & Southern Railway.

📍 ACQUISITION

MOSCHIP TECHNOLOGIES: Acquires 73% stake in Vayavya Labs for ₹245 Cr to strengthen semiconductor capabilities.

ANLON HEALTHCARE: Acquires 63.98% stake in Remember India Health Links for ₹5.38 Cr, expanding into formulations.

📍 COLLABORATION

SIGNATURE GLOBAL: Partners with Tonino Lamborghini for luxury housing project in Gurugram.

ECO HOTELS: Partners with Kesari Tours to boost spiritual tourism across key destinations.

ISGEC: Signs MoU with Nigeria for technical support in sugar plant development projects.

ADITYA INFOTECH: Forms 50:50 JV with Orient Cables to manufacture cables & connectors.

DCM SHRIRAM: Forms JV with Teknor Apex to expand specialty polymer & advanced materials portfolio.

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

LT FOODS: Appoints Ritesh Sud as Chief Marketing Officer for India & Far East markets.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

GAIL: To set up 600 MW solar project with BESS in Uttar Pradesh to support energy transition.

BPCL: Approves final investment for Brazil oil & gas project with ~$2.8B investment.

DODLA DAIRY: Subsidiary gets land allotment in Bihar to set up dairy processing plant.

📍 FUNDRAISING

WIPRO: Board approves ₹15,000 Cr share buyback via tender offer at ₹250/share.

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

HPL ELECTRIC & POWER: CRISIL upgrades rating to A+ / Stable, reaffirming strong growth outlook.

JAMNA AUTO: ICRA upgrades long-term rating to AA / Stable, improving credit profile.

HUDCO: Credit rating reaffirmed at AAA / Stable with strong government backing.

OMNITECH ENGINEERING: Receives CRISIL A- / Stable rating for ₹400 Cr bank facilities.

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

ALEMBIC PHARMA: Receives USFDA approval for Methotrexate Injection for US market.

ZYDUS LIFE: Gets USFDA EIR for oncology injectable facility in Ahmedabad.

📍 CLARIFICATION

RELIANCE POWER / INFRA: Clarifies no current association with former executives amid media reports.

HINDUSTAN COPPER: Denies active JV talks with CODELCO, clarifies only exploration cooperation exists.

📍 LAUNCHES

IKS HEALTH: Launches AI platform ‘MyCareHub’ to enhance patient engagement & financial metrics.

FIRSTSOURCE: Launches AI operating system ‘Kairos’ to drive enterprise automation.

AVI POLYMERS: Launches AI-based agri platform ‘KrishiBuddy’ marking shift to agri-tech.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

RASHI PERIPHERALS: Approves semiconductor subsidiary & Singapore entity with ₹80 Cr investment.

ENCOMPASS DESIGN INDIA: To set up wholly-owned subsidiaries in USA & UAE for global expansion.

AYM SYNTEX: Moves ahead with merger of Mandawewala Enterprises to simplify group structure.

AUTOLINE INDUSTRIES: Exits subsidiary AIPL by selling 47.8% stake to MNSC Realty.

📍 IT NEWS

INTELLECT DESIGN: OnCost Kuwait adopts eMACH.ai Retail platform to transform operations.

📍 ADDITIONS

VEGA JEWELLERS: Opens new retail store in Guntur, expanding presence to 6 stores.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Aditya Infotech (CP Plus) partners with Orient Cables to form a 50:50 JV for manufacturing electric cables and connectivity solutions  

STRUCTURAL SHIFT  
- Backward integration into cables and connectivity ecosystem  
- Expansion beyond surveillance hardware into infrastructure components  
- Increasing localization and control over supply chain  
- Entry into high-growth data and security infrastructure segment  
- Impact: Positive — ecosystem expansion  

WHY THIS MATTERS  
- Reduces dependency on third-party suppliers  
- Improves margins through integration  
- Captures additional value in surveillance + networking stack  
- Aligns with rising demand for data cables, CCTV infra, and connectivity solutions  
- Impact: Positive — margin and growth driver  

BUSINESS STRATEGY

JOINT VENTURE STRUCTURE  
- 50:50 JV between Aditya Infotech and Orient Cables  
- New private company to be incorporated  
- Agreement executed on 16 April 2026  
- Impact: Strategic partnership for manufacturing scale  

PRODUCT SCOPE  
- LAN cables  
- CCTV cables  
- Terminated assemblies  
- Connectors  
- Impact: Full-stack connectivity offering  

STRATEGIC RATIONALE

BACKWARD INTEGRATION  
- Control over key components used in surveillance solutions  
- Cost optimization and supply chain efficiency  
- Impact: Margin accretive  

MARKET OPPORTUNITY  
- Rising demand from data centers, enterprise networking, and security systems  
- Growth in smart cities and digital infrastructure  
- Impact: Strong long-term demand tailwind  

PARTNERSHIP SYNERGY  
- Aditya Infotech: Strong brand (CP Plus) and distribution  
- Orient Cables: Manufacturing expertise  
- Impact: Complementary strengths  

MOAT CHARACTERISTICS  
- Integrated product ecosystem (hardware + connectivity)  
- Strong distribution network  
- Manufacturing capability via JV  
- Increasing presence in infrastructure layer  
- Impact: Strengthening competitive positioning  

KEY INSIGHT  
- CP Plus evolving from a product company to an integrated surveillance and connectivity solutions provider  

OUTLOOK  
- JV to support long-term margin expansion and product diversification  
- Scale-up dependent on execution and demand traction  

IMPACT  
- Positive — strategic integration with strong synergy benefits and growth optionality

Commercial Vehicle Industry – FY27 Outlook:

• CV industry grew 11.7% YoY in FY26
• Strong rebound phase

• FY27 growth expected at ~4–6%
• Shift towards normalization

• Growth slowdown due to:
– Macro uncertainty
– Oil price volatility
– Monsoon risks
– Tighter credit conditions

• Segment outlook:
– LCV trucks: 3–5% growth
– M&HCV trucks: 5–7% growth
– Buses: 7–9% growth

• Buses likely to outperform
• Driven by public transport and infra push

• CV demand linked to:
– Infrastructure execution
– Freight movement
– Rural demand

• Industry entering steady growth phase
• Mid-single-digit trajectory expected

👉SMRs won’t create a new sector - they will supercharge high-spec manufacturing across forging, cables, valves, EPC & automation.

SMRs today are uranium-based, but they build the scale, plutonium pipeline & ecosystem needed for India’s long-term thorium (U-233) roadmap.

🔹Thorium = endgame (energy independence)

reports.text:
India’s Power Demand: Heat‑Driven Rebound From Mid‑May

Where demand stands today
- Peak demand in late March reached ~238 GW during solar hours and ~224.6 GW in non‑solar hours.  
- Q4FY26 saw peak demand touch ~245 GW, signalling underlying structural growth despite the muted run‑rate in FY26.  
- Power demand growth in FY26 is only about 0.8%, with March at 0.7%, reflecting a softer base and benign weather.  

Temporary cool‑off, not structural drop
- Unseasonal rains have pulled demand down to roughly 200–220 GW in some patches.  
- However, IEX expects a quick rebound from mid‑May as temperatures normalise and summer heats up.  
- El Niño‑driven hotter summers could push peak load toward ~240 GW or higher, particularly in cities and industrial belts where cooling and production drive load.  

Solar, prices, and thermal role
- Solar is temporarily outpacing part‑load demand, pulling IEX‑level prices down to a record low of ~₹0.0002 per unit during March’s peak‑solar hours.  
- Despite this, thermal‑plant load factors (PLFs) remain elevated, showing that coal and gas are still essential for balancing diurnal and peak loads.  

What the real risk is
- The danger is not weak demand growth, but how fast peaks are rising versus the pace at which India can add flexible, grid‑ready capacity.  
- We are now in a regime of higher baseload and sharper, more frequent peaks; the system‑stress trigger is “peak‑to‑spare‑capacity” compression, not just annual growth %.

Investment lens
- For power‑sector names, this environment supports thermal and transmission‑linked stories when prices spike, while renewables benefit from falling LCOE and higher utilisation over time.  
- Grid‑operators like IEX gain from higher volatility and volumes, but are also exposed to regulatory and pricing‑cap overhangs.

HDFC Securities Limited, the stock broking and financial distribution subsidiary of HDFC Bank, just released its Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results for the year ended 31 March 2026.

The headline numbers reflect the cyclical reality facing the entire Indian capital markets and broking industry — strong underlying fundamentals but a meaningful earnings dip driven by SEBI's derivatives regulations and softer trading volumes.

WIPRO LTD

Outlook (Q1FY27)
✓ IT Services Revenue expected $2,597–2,651 Mn

HDFC AMC Q4 FY26 Snapshot – Key Numbers

AUM and penetration
- Total QAAUM: ₹9,275 billion, up 20% YoY and flat QoQ; 11.4% share of MF‑industry QAAUM.  
- Total closing AUM: ₹8,440 billion, up 12% YoY, down 8% QoQ (market‑driven correction).  
- Equity‑oriented QAAUM: ₹5,657 billion, up 23% YoY and flat QoQ; strong participation in bull‑run SIPs.  

Distribution and flows
- Live accounts at 3.01 crore, up 30% YoY; broadening household‑investor base.  
- 16.5 million systematic transactions with ₹48.8 billion processed in March 2026, underlining active SIP culture.  
- 68% of AUM from individual investors vs ~60% industry average, reinforcing retail‑anchored franchise.  

Product mix
- Equity vs non‑equity QAAUM mix at 65:35, versus industry 56:44; higher equity tilt supports fee‑beta upside in rising markets.  

Financials – Q4 FY26
- Revenue: +17% YoY, –2% QoQ.  
- Other income: –91% YoY, –93% QoQ (largely timing and one‑offs).  
- Total income: +4% YoY, –14% QoQ.  
- Operating profit: +16% YoY, –4% QoQ.  
- PBT: flat YoY, –18% QoQ.  
- PAT: –2% YoY, –19% QoQ.  

Financials – FY26
- Revenue: +18% YoY.  
- Other income: –11% YoY.  
- Total income: +14% YoY.  
- Operating profit: +18% YoY.  
- PBT: +13% YoY.  
- PAT: +16% YoY.  
- ROE: 32.9% (high‑quality return on equity).  
- Operating margin (bps of AAUM) in Q1–Q4 FY26: 36, 35, 36, 35 – demonstrating stable operating leverage.  

Tone
- Underlying AUM and fee growth remain strong; the marginal PAT dip in Q4 is primarily driven by non‑core income and modest expense pressure rather than structural weakness.  
- High retail tilt, growing equity share, and strong live‑account growth sustain the multi‑year SIP‑driven growth story.

Wipro Q4 FY26 Snapshot – Key Numbers

Q4 FY26 Performance
- Gross revenue: ₹24,240 crore, up 7.7% YoY and 2.9% QoQ.  
- IT services revenue: $2,651 million, up 2.1% YoY and 0.6% QoQ.  
- In constant currency (CC): IT services revenue down 0.2% YoY, up 0.2% QoQ, indicating muted pricing but volume growth.  
- IT services operating margin: 17.3%, down 20 bps YoY and 30 bps QoQ, reflecting cost and mix pressure.  
- Net profit: ₹3,500 crore, down 1.9% YoY but up 12.3% QoQ.  
- Total bookings: $3.46 billion, up 3.2% QoQ in CC.  
- Large deal bookings: $1.44 billion, up 65% QoQ in CC, signalling strong future visibility.  
- Operating cash flow: ₹3,170 crore, or 90.1% of net profit, reaffirming healthy working‑capital discipline.  
- Attrition (12‑month rolling): 13.8%, within a manageable range versus prior peaks.

FY26 Full Year
- Gross revenue: ₹92,620 crore, up 4.0% YoY.  
- IT services revenue: $10,478 million, down 0.3% YoY, or –1.6% YoY in CC, reflecting macro‑related pressure.  
- Operating margin: 17.2%, up 20 bps YoY, showing incremental operating leverage.  
- Net profit: ₹13,200 crore, up 0.5% YoY.  
- Adjusted net profit: ₹13,430 crore, up 2.2% YoY.  
- Large deal bookings: $7.8 billion, up 45.4% YoY, and total bookings at $16.4 billion, up 14.0% YoY, building a multi‑year pipeline.  
- Operating cash flow: ₹14,930 crore, or 112.6% of net profit, reinforcing balance‑sheet quality.

Q1 FY27 Guidance
- IT services revenue guided at $2,597–2,651 million, implying QoQ growth of –2% to 0% in constant currency.  
- This suggests a stabilised but not sharply re‑accelerating top line near term.

Capital allocation
- Announced share buyback of ₹15,000 crore at ₹250 per share, signalling strong capital‑return confidence.

Management commentary
- Focus on AI‑led transformation and platform‑driven services to drive higher‑value revenue.  
- Strong deal wins across verticals point to a robust future growth pipeline.  
- Continued investment in capabilities and talent to support technology‑and‑AI transition.

Tone
- Underlying business is stabilising, with healthy bookings and margin resilience; the modest YoY PAT dip reflects a trough‑to‑plateau return‑to‑growth phase rather than structural stress.

In just 18 months of consolidation, 35 lakh people have surrendered.

To make money in the stock market, you need the heart of a tiger 🐅.

Not having to answer to investors or chase revenue targets is a huge advantage and, honestly, a blessing. It's what has allowed us to stick to our philosophies like not spamming users, not tracking behaviour, not having different pricing for different people, and not doing things that aren't in the interest of customers. All of this is easy to say and harder to stick with, especially when I look at the charts of listed brokers 😬

One example of us sticking to our philosophy: we still charge zero brokerage on equity delivery trades. We ran some back of the envelope math on how much 
at zerodha customers would have paid had we charged for it.

Multi-Asset Funds Shine! 🌟

Multi-asset funds (equities + gold + fixed income + International equities) delivered 11.4% annualised returns over 16 yrs, beating BSE Sensex TRI (10.7%). 

They lag pure gold (14.7%) & global equities (18.6%), but offer consistency. 

Multi-asset portfolios posted ZERO losses in any calendar year — unlike domestic equities, gold (losses in 4 yrs) or int'l equities (1 yr loss).

→ End-to-end manufacturing (machining → forging → assembly) in one ecosystem
→ High-complexity materials (titanium, nickel alloys)
→ Massive export focus (86% revenue from exports)
This creates high entry barriers.
Once you’re in… you don’t get replaced easily.

Add to that:
• 5,000+ aerospace parts portfolio
• Multi-country manufacturing clusters
• Strong execution track record (D2P, SQIP awards)

Simple takeaway:
Aequs isn’t chasing growth.
It’s building a moat through capability + relationships.

reports.text:
India EMS Opportunity: A Structural Manufacturing Shift

Global EMS landscape
- The global Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) market is ~$1.1–1.2 trillion, growing at 5–6% CAGR.  
- China dominates with ~30% share (~$350–370 billion), growing at 10–12%.  
- Other key hubs: Vietnam (China‑plus‑1 beneficiary), Mexico (nearshoring for US), Taiwan (high‑end electronics and semis).

India EMS: small, but accelerating
- India EMS is currently ~$55–60 billion (~₹5 lakh crore), about 4–5% of global EMS.  
- Growth is 30–35% CAGR, far above the global and Chinese averages.  
- By FY28, the market is expected to reach ~₹9 lakh crore (~$105–110 billion).  
- Key drivers: China+1, PLI schemes, rising domestic electronics demand, and export push.

Market structure: scarcity premium in listed names
- Only ~20% of India’s EMS market is listed.  
- The rest is private players and MNC captive units.  
- This creates a scarcity premium and earnings‑optionality for listed EMS stocks.

Segment mix and margin shift
- Mobile phones dominate India EMS at ~65% of revenue.  
- Consumer electronics is ~15%; the rest is auto, industrial, IT hardware, and telecom.  
- Margins by segment:  
  - Consumer electronics: 4–5% EBITDA.  
  - Mobiles: low single‑digit, very high‑volume.  
  - Auto electronics: high single‑digit.  
  - EV electronics: low double‑digit.  
  - Industrials: 15–16% (highest).  
- The structural trend is clear: shift from low‑margin mobile assembly to higher‑value industrial, auto, EV, aerospace, and defence.

Key listed players and value‑chain move
- Kaynes Technology India: strong in industrials, auto, and aerospace; high‑engineering content.  
- Syrma SGS Technology: expanding into auto and industrial electronics.  
- Avalon Technologies: high export share, strong in industrials and automotive.  
- Dixon Technologies: leader in mobile + consumer electronics, now adding PCB and components.  
- Amber Enterprises India: strong in ACs and consumer durables, moving into PCB/PCBA and in‑house content.  
- The direction is: EMS → design‑engineering → PCB/PCBA → component content → margin expansion.

PCB: the missing localization layer
- India PCB market is ~₹36,000 crore, with 85–90% dependence on imports.  
- New capacity announcements and EMS‑player backward integration are addressing this gap.  
- Government incentives and PLI‑style logic make PCB the next big import‑substitution opportunity.  

OSAT: the semiconductor frontier
- Current India OSAT (chip assembly and test) market is only ~₹300 crore.  
- It is expected to grow to ~₹2,000 crore in 2–3 years.  
- Key entrants: Kaynes, CG Power, Tata Group, Micron.  
- OSAT is critical to build a full semiconductor ecosystem and reduce import dependence.

Key themes to track
1. China+1 tailwind: Global OEMs are diversifying; India is gaining share in electronics exports.  
2. Backward integration: EMS → PCB → semiconductor packaging and OSAT.  
3. Margin expansion: Shift from mobile to industrials, EV, aerospace, defence, and medical.  
4. Export move: India is moving from a domestic‑assembly hub to a global‑manufacturing‑hub story.  

What to watch going forward
- PCB capacity announcements and yield/quality ramp‑up.  
- OSAT execution and utilisation timelines.  
- Share of mobile revenue in EMS companies, and progress on diversification.  
- Export‑order wins in auto, industrial, EV, and defence‑electronics.  
- Design‑led manufacturing and ODM‑type contracts versus pure assembly.  

Final take
- India EMS is in the very early innings of a multi‑year manufacturing upcycle.  
- The global market is large but mature; China is dominant but slowing.  
- India is small but compounding at 30%+.  
- The real story is not just volume growth, but moving from “assembling phones” to owning the design, PCB, and semiconductor‑assembly layers of the value chain.

reports.text:
GAIL (India) – 600 MW Solar + 550 MWh BESS Project (Positive)

- GAIL has approved a 600 MW solar project in Uttar Pradesh, co‑located with a 550 MWh battery energy storage system (BESS).  
- The project is being set up at the TUSCO Solar Park in Jhansi, with an estimated investment of about ₹3,295 crore.  
- Power from the plant will be used primarily for GAIL’s petrochemical and gas projects, including its captive needs at the Pata petrochemical complex.  
- Commissioning is targeted within 15 months from the EPC‑contract award.  

Impact
- The project will sharply lift GAIL’s installed renewable capacity from about 147 MW today to well over 1,000 MW once this and other green‑field solar projects are online.  
- It strengthens GAIL’s positioning as an integrated energy major with a balanced mix of gas, petrochemicals, and renewables.  
- The 550 MWh BESS will improve grid stability and help manage solar intermittency for captive‑use loads.  

Sentiment
- Overall impact is positive: clear step‑up in capex toward clean energy and decarbonisation, enhancing long‑term earnings visibility and ESG profile.

**CLSA PRICE ACTION – NIFTY TECHNICAL VIEW (LAURENCE BALANCO)**

- After forming a price and momentum divergence into the March lows, Nifty has staged a sharp rebound.  
- The current recovery is now testing overhead resistance at 24,300–24,400.  
- This zone coincides with the 50DMA and the lower boundary of the May–September 2025 trading range.  
- A decisive break above 24,300–24,400 would place the index back into the H2CY25 trading band.  
- The 200DMA sits as the next upside hurdle near 25,130.  
- Meaningful overhead resistance runs along the upper edge of the 2024‑to‑date range at 26,270–26,340.  
- Until then, some consolidation around current levels is consistent with digesting the recent sharp rebound.

reports.text:
Power Demand to Rebound from Mid-May; Peak Load May Cross 240 GW

India’s power demand expected to surge from mid-May, driven by rising temperatures

El Niño conditions likely to push peak demand beyond 240 GW

March saw temporary dip due to unseasonal rains and lower temperatures

Peak demand earlier(March 10) touched 238 GW (solar hours) and 224.6 GW (non-solar)

Sudden weather changes caused 40/20 GW drop in demand within 10 days

Peak load fell to ~200–220 GW, impacting electricity prices

IEX prices dropped sharply to ₹0.0002/unit, lowest since inception

Expected quick recovery in demand and power prices from mid-May

Q4FY26 peak demand already hit ~245 GW, indicating strong underlying demand

Thermal plant load factors at ~69.8% in March, leaving room for ramp-up

Solar oversupply during daytime pressured prices, while evening prices rose

Power demand growth remained muted in FY26 (~0.8% YoY)

Weather patterns and monsoon outlook (90–95% of normal) key for demand trends

Structural demand drivers remain intact despite short-term volatility

**MOTILAL OSWAL ON ICICI LOMBARD**

**Maintain Buy, with target price of ₹2,230, down from ₹2,270 earlier.  **

- ICICI Lombard beat on combined ratio, but tepid investment income dragged earnings.  
- Motor segment is recovering and continues to gain market share in health.  
- 2HFY26 showed better‑than‑industry growth, led by strong motor and retail‑health performance.  
- NEP for FY26 cut by 2% and FY27 by 3%.  
- PAT for FY26 cut by 6% and FY27 by 2%.  
- Management expects combined ratio to improve to ~101.8% by FY28, helped by a stable claims environment and higher operational efficiency.

India’s March Exports Drop 7.4% Amid West Asia Shock, FY26 Still Marginally Positive

Merchandise exports fell 7.4% YoY to $38.91 bn in March

Sharp impact from West Asia shipments plunging ~58%

Disruption caused by US–Israel–Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz blockage

Exports to West Asia dropped to ~$2.5 bn vs usual ~$6 bn/month

Imports also declined 6.5% YoY to $59.59 bn

Trade deficit remained relatively contained at ~$20.7 bn

March marked first export contraction since Oct 2025

Overall goods trade activity weakened due to geopolitical disruptions

Despite volatility, FY26 exports grew marginally ~0.9%

Indicates resilience in annual performance despite late-year pressure

Consumption  
- March retail sales up 1.7% YoY vs 2.8% previous and 2.4% estimate – clear slowdown in consumer spending, domestic demand still fragile.  

Jobs  
- March unemployment rate at 5.4% vs 5.3% previous and 5.2% estimate – labour market showing mild stress, not easing yet.  

Industry  
- March industrial production up 5.7% YoY vs 6.3% previous and 5.4% estimate – still expanding, but pace has moderated.  

Market View  
- Data mix: GDP beat, but soft consumption, higher unemployment and cooling industry – not an “all clear” for a big China demand comeback.  
- Keep an eye on Asian equities, commodities, metals and China‑linked names; moves there can influence Indian cyclicals and export-oriented stocks.

Mark Mobius, who championed emerging markets, long before it became fashionable to do, has passed away.

Mobius was was known for his hands-on, on-the-ground investment philosophy. Over the course of his career, he visited over a 100 countries in search of undervalued companies.

Mobius retired from Franklin Templeton in 2018 after more than three decades with the firm - during which period - Templeton's EM assets went from $100m to $400B. 

I have been speaking with Mark for over 20 years & he was always polite, generous with his time & insights. He remained a firm believer in the India story - both the economy & it's companies.

Rest well, Mark !

Groww_Initiating Coverage by BofA_15042026.pdf
Groww_Initiating Coverage by BofA_15042026.pdf

Why 27th April is imp for Iran ?

Iran’s oil system is built for flow—not storage.

A) It produces ~3.2 million barrels per day, consumes ~1.7 million, leaving roughly 1.5 million barrels per day with nowhere to go if exports are disrupted.

B) Onshore storage sits around 50–55 million barrels and is already ~60% full. That leaves ~20 million barrels of headroom—barely two weeks of buffer.

C) After that, Iran doesn’t just lose exports—it faces a harder choice: shut in wells.

And in mature fields, shutting in production isn’t reversible like flipping a switch. Water intrusion can damage reservoirs, meaning when wells restart, they produce less oil—and sometimes mostly water.

Estimates suggest prolonged shut-ins could risk hundreds of thousands of barrels per day in lasting production loss.

This isn’t just a supply disruption. It’s potential supply destruction.

HSBC Research Initiating Coverage on ACME Solar Holdings, with 28% UPSIDE- Initiate at Buy: Putting batteries to good use

**Antique on **

Initiating Coverage on ACME Solar Holdings: BUY | TP 363 | Powering India’s Dispatchable Clean Energy Future

ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company: BUY | TP 3,650 | Core strength intact with flows and scale drive earnings

ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company: BUY | TP 700 | Better product mix drives margin outperformance

Thermax: HOLD | TP 4,152 | Strong Order Momentum Priced In; Maintain HOLD

Ujjivan Small Finance Bank: BUY | TP 70 | Outlook remains sanguine despite rejection of universal banking license

CPI: Expecting a limited upside risk to inflation!

**India’s households hold 34,600 tonnes of gold**

65% of non property wealth is in gold
Value is 175% higher than bank deposits and equities combined

Channelising just 2% annually could add $7.5 trillion to GDP by 2047

Largest idle asset pool in the economy turning into growth potential

Valuations Cooling Off. Opportunity Rising.

• Sensex P/E at 20.2x — lowest since May 2020
• Down from ~23.5x levels just weeks ago
• Sharp FPI selling driving multiple compression
• Markets correcting faster than earnings
• Near long-term median (~19–20x) → valuation comfort zone
• Historically, such phases create better entry setups

Less about timing peaks. More about spotting value.

JEFFERIES ON ADANI ENTERPRISES

BROKERAGE RADAR

Target: ₹2,600 (≈21% upside)

Recommendation: Buy (Maintain)

• 🚀 Growth Outlook: Strong FY27 ramp-up expected across multiple business verticals.

• ⚡️ New Energy: Benefits from energy security tailwinds and policy support.

• ☀️ Solar Expansion: Mundra capacity expansion likely to drive EBITDA growth from FY27.

• 🛢 Diversification: Copper and Roads businesses also expected to scale up in FY27.

• 📈 Overall View: Multi-business growth visibility with improving earnings trajectory.
MOTILAL OSWAL ASSIGNS BUY RATING TO ICICI PRUDENTIAL LIFE, TARGET ₹650 — RESEARCH NOTE DATED 15 APR 2026.

Asian Paints, Pidilite, Jyothy Labs and HUL In Focus – 10+% price hike needed to offset increase in raw material costs

Among pure play FMCG names, HUL and Jyothy Labs have seen an increase of 19% and 27% in their raw material cost

As per Kotak Securities, HUL and Jyothy Labs would require a price hike of 11% and 15%

Among consumer names, Asian Paints and Pidilite have seen an increase of 27% and 33% in raw material cost

As per Kotak Securities, Asian Paints and Pidilite need a price hike of 18% and 17%

Pidilite In Focus – Negative

Double Downgrade by BofA

BofA on Pidilite
Downgrade to Underperform from Buy; Cut TP to Rs 1375 from Rs 1630

See risks of slower global growth, cost push & relatively high valuation
Direct + indirect impact of the Middle East disruption may be underestimated by the Street
Direct and indirect impact of likely slower domestic growth, exposure to Middle East and export dependent industries
Expected cost pressures with rise in prices of VAM, oil derivatives, etc. and weaker INR

Axis Cap on Ujjivan SFB

Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 77
Universal bank license rejection a temporary setback.

Annoucement will have negative reaction on the stock price
Believe this to be a deferral, not a denial for the transition to an universal bank.

See this outcome as a regulatory pause, not a rejection, with a clear message to demonstrate more diversification.

Underlying business remains intact

Investors should view this as a temporary timeline shift rather than a structural negative

The universal banking licence, when granted, remains a meaningful re-rating catalyst

ELARA CAPITAL ON JINDAL SAW

Initiate Buy with a target price of 280

Earnings recovery expected over FY27-FY28, driven by water and oil & gas demand

Order book at all-time high (~1.4x trailing volumes), indicating strong visibility

Jal Jeevan Mission spending revival to drive domestic pipe demand

Global pipeline capex supported by energy security concerns

Exports rising, with diversification reducing dependence on domestic capex

Capacity expansion to support margins and operating leverage

Valuations attractive, with rerating potential driven by execution and growth

ELARA CAPITAL ON VISHAL MEGA MART

Initiate Buy with a target price of 130

Diversified model combining apparel, FMCG and general merchandise drives footfall and margins

Private label mix ~75%, enabling strong pricing power and higher margins

Revenue and EBITDA expected to grow at 18% and 21% CAGR over FY26-FY28

Asset-light model and low-cost structure drive superior return ratios

Strong store expansion runway, focused on tier 2 and smaller cities

Operating leverage to drive margin expansion to ~10.3% by FY28

Risk from potential supply overhang due to promoter stake dilution.
Mirae Asset Sharekhan Morning Tiger ( Pre - Market Insight ) - 15th April 2026.pdf
MOSL remains bullish on Hero MotoCorp, maintains Buy with target price at ₹6,205
Nuvama on Indian Economy: Most core categories like healthcare, education, transport saw broadly stable inflation trends

**Pine Labs Pvt Ltd – Citi View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹235

Payments fintech on **monetization improvement trajectory**
Strong positioning in **merchant payments ecosystem**

Large players relatively insulated due to:
Profitability focus across industry
Higher regulatory & compliance barriers
Strong **enterprise integration → high stickiness**

Competitive churn lower in **merchant payments vs consumer payments**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — improving monetization + strong competitive moat

**Dr. Reddy's Laboratories Ltd – Citi View:**

Recommendation: Sell
Target Price: ₹1,070

Semaglutide generic (Embeltah) **rejected by Brazil’s ANVISA**
Reason: did not meet **efficacy, safety & quality requirements**

Misses initial wave of **Brazil generic opportunity** (high competition with ~16 filings)
Apotex US approval may give it **lead in Canada market**

**Impact:** 🔴 Negative — regulatory setback impacts growth opportunity in key markets

**CEMENT SECTOR – Jefferies View:**

Elevated energy prices (West Asia tensions) → **key earnings risk**
Cost shocks difficult to **pass through in near term**

Estimated **₹300/t cost inflation (4QFY26–2QFY27)**
Only partial price hikes → **EBITDA cuts of ~4–9% (FY27–28)**

**Preferred Picks:**
**UltraTech Cement Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹14,025

**JK Cement Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹6,705

**Other Calls:**

**Ambuja Cements Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹615 (cut from ₹735)

**Shree Cement Ltd**
Hold | Target Price: ₹27,530 (cut)

**Dalmia Bharat Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹2,500 (cut)

**Impact:** 🔴 Negative — cost pressures likely to hit margins despite pricing actions

**METALS – HSBC View:**

Supply-side challenges from **Middle East conflict** (especially aluminium)
LME Aluminium forecasts **↑ 10% / 8% for CY26 / CY27**
Price forecasts at **$3,524 / $3,500/t (CY26–27)**
Long-term average seen at **~$3,300/t till CY30**

**Stock Calls:**

**Hindalco Industries Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹1,310

**National Aluminium Company Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹455

**Hindustan Zinc Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹720

**Tata Steel Ltd**
Buy | Target Price: ₹250

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — supply constraints + higher price outlook support metals cycle

**ALUMINIUM – JPMorgan View:**

LME aluminium prices **↑ ~6% to ~$3,687/t** (recent rally)
Driven by **Hormuz disruption risk → potential smelter shutdowns (alumina shortage)**
Ceasefire hopes reduce **demand-side risks**

2026 aluminium deficit estimated at **~1.9 MT (highest in 26 years)**
2QCY26 price outlook at **~$3,800/t**

Even de-escalation could be **bullish** (removes macro risks, supply still tight)
Sustained smelter outages = **further upside risk to prices**

**Preferred Picks:**
**Hindalco Industries Ltd**
**Vedanta Ltd**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong pricing cycle + supply tightness supports earnings

**ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company – Morgan Stanley View:**

Recommendation: Equal Weight
Target Price: ₹3,320

Operating revenue **in line with estimates**
Operating profit **↑ 4%** (cost reversals)

MF inflow trends **remain strong**
Positive on **franchise strength**, but valuation seen as **full**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — strong fundamentals, but valuation caps upside

**ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company – UBS View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹3,900 (↑)

Core performance **strong**; headline earnings impacted by **MTM losses**
Franchise momentum intact; **equity & hybrid segments outperform**

Margins **improving**; near-term yield headwinds manageable

FY27E/FY28E PAT estimates **↑ 4.9% / 2.2%**
Driven by higher **equity AUM** (adjusted for lower PMS/advisory AUM)

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong franchise + earnings upgrade trend

**ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company – HSBC View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹3,800 (↑)

Q4FY26 PAT **beat estimates** (treasury gains + cost discipline)
AUM market share gains remain **positive**

Earnings supported by **yield levers & operating leverage**
Strong outlook for **FY27–29 earnings outperformance**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — consistent AUM growth + operating leverage tailwinds

**ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company – Citi View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹3,900

Q4 core PBT at **₹11.3 bn (↑ 2% QoQ, in-line)**
AAUM growth sluggish QoQ due to **weak equity sentiment & mix shift**

MF yields saw **marginal moderation**
Lower employee cost supported **core profitability**

Flows remained **strong (flow share > AAUM share in equities)**
Focus on expanding **SIF & alternates** to drive growth

Impact of **expense ratio cut minimal (~3–4 bps)**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong flows + diversification support growth despite near-term yield pressure

**ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company – CLSA View:**

Recommendation: Outperform
Target Price: ₹3,730 (↑)

Q4 PAT at **₹7.6 bn** (below ₹8–9 bn run-rate due to MTM loss)
Ex-MTM: **operating profit ↑ ~2% QoQ**

MF QAAUM at **₹11 tn (↑ 3% QoQ | ↑ 26% YoY)**
Active equity AUM **↑ 2% QoQ**; market share **↑ 40 bps**

Industry flows remain **strong (SIP + lump-sum)**
No slowdown seen in **equity sales (April commentary)**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong AUM growth + resilient flows despite macro headwinds

**ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Ltd – UBS View:**

Recommendation: Neutral
Target Price: ₹760

Retail protection growth driving **margin expansion**
APE **↑ 2% YoY**; VNB **↑ 11% YoY (FY26)**

Embedded Value & VNB progression steady
Agency channel weak; **banca growth muted**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — margin strength, but growth moderation in key channels

**ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Ltd – Bernstein View:**

Recommendation: Market Perform
Target Price: ₹700

Q4 new business profit **strong**, but top-line growth **modest**
Margins supported by **mix shift, efficiencies & favorable yield curve**

Management did not provide **FY27 guidance**; focus on profitability
Transition to **Ind AS accounting from FY28**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — strong margins, but growth visibility moderate

**ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Ltd – Morgan Stanley View:**

Recommendation: Overweight
Target Price: ₹670

VNB & VNB margin **beat estimates** (retail protection + yield tailwinds)
Negatives: **operating variance, assumption changes, persistency**

Valuation at **~9x NTM P/VNB** for ~10% VNB CAGR (FY26–28E)
Seen as **attractive vs consensus expectations**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — valuation comfort + margin strength

**ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Ltd – HSBC View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹690

Q4FY26 performance **beat expectations on growth & margins**
RoEV impacted due to **unfavourable assumption changes**

Growth expected to **pick up with stable margins**
Re-rating dependent on **reduction in assumption volatility**

Valuation attractive at **~1.2x FY28E EV | ~3.8x FY28E VNB**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong growth outlook with reasonable valuations

**Indus Towers Ltd – Jefferies View:**

Recommendation: Underperform (Downgrade)
Target Price: ₹375 (from ₹530)

Downgrade due to **site renewal risks** (2HCY26–1HCY27) impacting growth
Elevated **capex (growth + maintenance)** to pressure earnings & FCF

Revenue/PAT estimates cut by **~2–6%**
Post-cut outlook: **~3% EPS growth | ~4% yield**

Growth risks likely to **limit re-rating potential**

**Impact:** 🔴 Negative — growth uncertainty + capex pressure weighs on outlook

**Abbott India Ltd – JPMorgan View:**

Recommendation: Neutral (Downgrade)
Target Price: ₹27,500

Lower target multiple due to **growth challenges ahead**
Limited new product launches; **Mixtard phase-out** to impact growth
Long-term risk from **GLP-1 therapies**

Management churn: **3 MD resignations in 4 years** → strategy uncertainty

Valuation now ~**31x/28x FY27E/FY28E P/E**, but growth remains subdued
Target multiple cut to **30x (from 37x)**, aligned with MNC peers

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — valuation reasonable, but growth visibility weak

**Billionbrains Garage Ventures – BoFA Securities View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹235

Well positioned to benefit from **India’s retail investing tailwinds**
Revenue expected to grow at **~30% CAGR (FY26–28)**

Best-in-class profitability; operating leverage to drive
**EBITDA margin ~67% | PAT margin ~52% by FY28E**

Valuation at **~39x FY28E P/E**

Near-term risks:
Weak capital market performance
**6-month lock-in expiry**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — strong growth + high profitability profile

**Ujjivan Small Finance Bank – Kotak Institutional Equities View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹72

RBI returned **universal bank license application** (needs more portfolio diversification)
No concerns flagged on **governance, compliance or operations**

Investment thesis **did not factor in conversion benefit**
Maintain Buy; monitor **changes in asset mix strategy**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — regulatory setback, but core fundamentals intact
Investec maintains buy rating on L&T

reports.text: Systematix Update on India Economy & Strategy- El Niño Threatens Monsoon, War Fuels Fertilizer Shortages – Somber Rural Outlook

MONARCH SURVEYORS & ENGINEERING CONSULTANTS (MCAP: ₹307 CR)

• Secures ~₹130 Cr railway consultancy order from Northern Railway
• Largest order till date → strong order book boost

• FY25 revenue: ₹154 Cr | Order size almost equivalent to annual revenue

• Total order book: ₹650 Cr+ → execution visibility over next 36 months

• Available at <10x P/E → attractive valuation with strong growth visibility

• Business trend: H2 is usually much stronger than H1 (last year ₹50 Cr vs ₹103 Cr) → management expects similar trend ahead

• Direct play on India’s infra capex (₹11L+ Cr spend) → benefits early via consultancy model

• End-to-end services (survey, DPR, design, land, PMC) → high margins + strong client stickiness

• Strong order book with multi-year visibility + continuous govt project pipeline

• Govt-focused clientele → stability, scale + repeat business

• High-margin profile: EBITDA ~30% | PAT ~19.5% → strong operating leverage

• Asset-light model → low capex, high ROCE potential, low debt (D/E ~0.18)

reports.text:
CITI ON ICICI PRU AMC

• Recommendation: Buy.

• Target Price: ₹3,900.

• Business Positioning: Seen as a mature, agile and diversified play in the Indian AMC space.

• Market Strength: Holds strong position in high-yielding mutual fund segment.

• Earnings Outlook: ~21% EPS CAGR expected over medium term, supported by robust inflows.

• Key Risks: Equity market volatility, performance cycles and higher distributor payouts may impact margins.

• Impact: Positive

CITI ON PINE LABS

• Recommendation: Positive / Constructive View.

• Target Price: ₹235.

• Growth Drivers: Strong opportunities in product expansion and distribution growth.

• Positioning: Well placed as a corporate fintech solutions provider.

• Financial Outlook: Revenue CAGR ~17% and EBITDA CAGR ~49% expected (FY26–FY28).

• Impact: Positive

JEFFERIES ON ICICI PRU AMC

• Recommendation: Buy.

• Target Price: ₹3,770 (Earlier: ₹3,600).

• Q4 Performance: Earnings beat driven by lower expenses.

• Defensive Play: Seen as relatively resilient in volatile markets.

• Cost Outlook: New ESOPs may increase employee costs, leading to 1–3% earnings cuts in FY27–28.

• Portfolio Strength: Lower SMID exposure + gains in net equity market share.

• Valuation View: Indian AMCs trading at premium to global peers due to higher profitability and growth.

• Impact: Positive

BofA On CPI

BROKERAGE RADAR

Prices broadly contained with limited pass through

CPI Details: Food prices higher, services prices contained

WPI to see higher pass-through of commodity price increases

Pass-through on inflation is likely to come with a lag

Continue to expect CPI at 5.2% for FY27

Continue to expect 50bps hike starting December 2026

Kotak Securities on CPI

BROKERAGE RADAR

Climate and geopolitical risks weighing on inflation

Expect RBI to remain on a prolonged pause in CY2026

FY27 CPI estimated at 4.7%

See chances of a rate hike arising under significant upside risks to the medium-term inflation trajectory

Citi On CPI

BROKERAGE

RADAR

Headline inflation reached a 14-month high

Revise FY27 headline inflation forecast to 4.6%

Continue to believe that the RBI can stay on hold for the immediate future

Risk of a modest upward adjustment to policy rate will arise if core inflation stays elevated above ~4.5% due to supply shocks

Macquarie On India Fuel Retail

BROKERAGE RADAR

Significant under-recoveries; brace for higher pump prices

At spot price, India's oil marketing companies are effectively subsidising consumers by Rs18/Rs35 per litre on petrol/diesel

While the contribution of excise to government revenue has been declining, it still forms a meaningful ~8% of revenue and ~17% of the fiscal deficit

Top down, see risk of higher pump prices post state elections in April, which will likely pressure margins

Given near-term earnings uncertainty, prefer utilities to oil marketing companies

reports.text: Systematix Update on India Economy & Strategy- El Niño Threatens Monsoon, War Fuels Fertilizer Shortages – Somber Rural Outlook

China has massive economic exposure to the Middle East:

China has spent over $269 billion on investments and construction contracts across the Middle East since 2005.

Saudi Arabia is the largest recipient at $82 billion, followed by the UAE at $48 billion, and Iraq at $40 billion.

Furthermore, Chinese investments and construction contracts in Iran have reached $25 billion.

Meanwhile, Middle East trade with China has more than doubled since 2017, to $317 billion in 2024.

That is nearly 4 times the $85 billion in trade with the US in 2024.

China is heavily tied to the Middle East's economy.

Source: The Kobeissi Letter

The Indian paints market looks crowded on the surface

But when you map every player across every category luxury to economy, interior to exterior the gaps become very obvious

BOSCH – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

• Acquisition to enable localization of solutions and better cater to market demand

• Bosch Chassis Systems holds a dominant market presence in India in its segments

• Stringent safety norms in India expected to drive strong growth opportunities

• Rising demand for premium cars leading to higher focus on safety features

• Completion of the acquisition targeted by Q1FY27

BOSCH ANALYST CALL

Product Portfolio Of Bosch Chassis Is Largely Powertrain Agnostic

Seeing Enhanced Regulations For Safety In India

Tightening Safety Norms Provide Long-term Growth Visibility

Bosch Chassis Demonstrated Steady Growth, 17% Rev CAGR Since FY23

Bosch Chassis Acqn Is A Strategic Move

Bosch Chassis Can Based On Key Benefits Including Enhanced Portfolio, Financial AccretionCopied from stockaajorkal telegram channel 

Bosch Chassis Brings Highly Profitable Business, Will Accelerate Co's Growth

Acqn Will Empower Co To Lead Future Of Mobility

🚘⚡️ India’s EV Ecosystem Play – Key Listed Companies by Segment 👇

Any new stocks comment below 

🔹 Silicon Carbide – Carborundum, Grindwell Norton
🔹 Battery – Exide, Amara Raja, Kabra
🔹 Charging Infra – Tata Power, Reliance, BPCL
🔹 Chemicals – Tata Chemicals, Gujarat Fluoro
🔹 PET Film – Uflex, Polyplex, Cosmo Films
🔹 Commercial Vehicles – Ashok Leyland, Olectra Greentech, M&M
🔹 2W & 4W – Tata Motors, TVS, Bajaj Auto, Hero
🔹 Auto Ancillaries – Sona BLW, Minda, Suprajit
🔹 Commodities – NALCO, Hindalco, Hindustan Copper
🔹 Software (ER&D) – Tata Elxsi, KPIT, L&T Tech

🚀 The entire EV value chain is listed & growing!

ITC LIMITED – NUVAMA VIEW

• West Asia crisis likely to weigh on Q4 agri business

• FMCG business expected to grow around 10%

• Cigarette volumes likely to remain under pressure in H1FY27

• Next two quarters expected to be challenging for cigarette companies

• Worst likely behind for ITC with valuations appearing comfortable

• Paint companies may see another round of price hikes in Q1

• Positive view on Titan Company, Avenue Supermarts and V-Mart Retail

• Underweight stance maintained on QSR space

MS on MCX

Upgrade to Overweight from Equal-weight; Hike TP to Rs 3270 from Rs 2550

Business model benefits from elevated commodity price volatility

This is currently being driven by geopolitical uncertainty

Exchange volumes rise with heightened volatility in commodity prices

Well-positioned as a defensive play
See strong revenue growth in the current risk-off environment

Bernstein On Anand Rathi Wealth

Have concerns around the ESOP costs & the offsetting revaluation

Q1/Q2/Q3FY27 reported P&L will likely show a negative/weak profit growth

Co has issued ESOPs to key management personnel worth 1.5% of total company
Target at ₹3,280/sh

**One 97 Communications Ltd (Paytm) – Haitong View:**

Recommendation: Outperform  
Target Price: ₹1,410  

Well placed to benefit from **rising digital payments adoption** in India  
Strong focus on **SME merchant digitization driving monetization**  
Structural levers to improve **payments margin (MDR mix + product innovation)**  

Strong moat in **merchant lending (large on-ground network)**  
Operating leverage to drive **core EBITDA margin to ~17% by FY28E**  

Core EBITDA & PAT expected to **grow ~4x (FY26–28E)**  
ROE likely to improve to **~12% by FY28E**  

Impact: Positive

Macquarie On India Consumer Durables

C&W to continue to deliver growth

Cable and wire industry volume growth estimated at 15%+ in 4QFY26

Summer season key for cooling products, factor 15%+ FY27 volume growth

Commodity prices increase may lead to margin risk

Price hikes of 10-15% since Jan-26 taken to offset cost inflation

Polycab remains top pick; Crompton, Havells, Polycab rated Outperform

Voltas rated Neutral

Axis Capital on Ujjivan SFB

Target Price ₹77

Earlier Target ₹66

Recommendation Buy

Secured for success

Transition to diversified universal banker contender

Secured mix improving (~49%) & targeting 65%-70% by FY30

Strong growth: loans +26% YoY; disbursements at record highs

Asset quality has normalised; collections near ~99.8%

CASA to improve

Capital raise likely, to support next growth phase

JPMorgan on Housing Finance

LIC Housing - Downgrade to Neutral from Overweight; Cut Target Price to Rs 580 from Rs 600

Bajaj Housing - Initiate Underweight with Target Price of Rs 70

PNB Housing - Initiate Overweight with Target Price of Rs 1000

Lenders with strong liability franchise or distribution have been gaining market share, which should continue

Intense competition in prime mortgages by PSBs suggests profitability is close to peak

This is forcing HFCs to explore growth in high yield segments

Prefer housing financiers with strong underwriting, low cost distribution and ability to deliver market share gains

Bajaj Housing stands out, however, premium to peers for similar profitability seems unsustainable

PNB Housing is in early years of its turnaround and offers growth at a reasonable price

LIC Housing may continue to lose market share and lacks immediate catalysts, but low valuations limit downside

ICICI Securities On Sumitomo Chemicals

Initiate Buy with target price of Rs 515

Unique positioning, solid credentials and reasonable valuation provide material upside

Symbiotic dominance: Global innovator parent, local edge - an unparalleled moat

Aggressive capex plans to support growth aspirations

Product launches to support momentum

Indian agriculture demand to remain robust for next 12-18 months

**UPL Ltd – Morgan Stanley View:**

Recommendation: Equal Weight
Target Price: ₹658

Play on **revival in global crop chemical volumes**
Delivered **2–2.5x higher volume growth vs global peers** (last 8 quarters)

Constructive on agchem cycle, but **near-term uncertainty persists**
Risks: **supply dislocations, feedstock sourcing, demand volatility**

Well positioned to benefit from **industry recovery cycle**
Expected to deliver **3–6% volume growth + market share gains (FY27–28)**

Impact: Mixed — strong positioning, but near-term uncertainties remain

BERNSTEIN ON ANAND RATHI WEALTH

BERNSTEIN SAYS

WE HAVE CONCERNS AROUND THE ESOP COSTS & THE OFFSETTING REVALUATION GAINS

THE EXPECTED ESOP CHARGES TO THE P&L OVER THE NEXT 15 QUARTERS WILL BE ~INR 3.5 BN, BEFORE TAX (~14% OF FY27/28/29 COMBINED PROFIT FORECAST)

THIS WILL CREATE A MEANINGFUL DRAG ON EARNINGS GROWTH FOR THE COMPANY

**Avenue Supermarts Ltd – Emkay View:**

Recommendation: Sell
Target Price: ₹3,700
Bear Case: ₹2,200

Valuation rich at **~70x forward P/E** (pricing future potential)
Quick commerce gap narrowing: only **4–13% premium vs DMart**
Bill cuts/sqft **↓ 6% since FY20** despite store expansion

Capex/store **↑ 60%**; RoIC declining (**~19% → ~13.5% by FY28E**)
Covers only **~50% of India retail TAM** (growth still evolving)
PEG at **4.2x vs peers (Trent, Titan ~2.6x)** → expensive

Impact: Negative — valuation concerns + rising competitive pressure

Morgan Stanley On Financials

FY27 Outlook Was Initially Strong

Geopolitical Risks May Disrupt Momentum

Lower Valuations Provide Downside Cushion

Maintain Positive 12-18 Month View

Prefer To Avoid Mid-sized Private Banks, PSU Banks & Expensive Stocks

**Coal India Ltd – Citi View:**

Recommendation: Neutral
Target Price: ₹430 (CMP: ₹434.10)

Company likely to **absorb higher costs** from West Asia crisis
Explosives costs **↑ 26%**; diesel costs **↑ 54%**
These inputs ~8% of costs → **overall cost impact ~3%**

Some units cutting **e-auction reserve prices** while increasing volumes
Discount to import parity may **widen beyond ~30% average**

Impact: Mixed — cost pressure + pricing headwinds offset volume push

**AUTO SECTOR – Nomura View (CAFE 2027):**

Policy direction clearly **pro-EV**
April’26 draft introduces **key changes vs Sep’25**
Hybrid super-credit reduced from **2.0x → 1.6x**
**3g CO₂/km benefit removed** for small cars
Derogation tech may **reduce EV mix by 2–4%**

Non-compliance penalty at **₹2,500/g (FY28)** → rising to **₹4,500/g (FY32)**
Policy likely to **accelerate EV adoption**

**Preferred Picks:**
**Mahindra & Mahindra Ltd**
**Hyundai Motor India Ltd**
**Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd**

**Impact:** 🟢 Positive — structural push towards EV ecosystem

**HFC SECTOR – JPMorgan View:**

Mortgage growth seen at **~8–10% (3–5 years)**; currently ~9%
Growth slowdown due to **weak residential sales**
PSBs & strong distributors **gaining market share**
Prime mortgage profitability near peak → shift to **higher-yield segments**
Preference for **strong underwriting + low-cost distribution players**

**Stock Calls:**

**PNB Housing Finance Ltd**
Overweight | Target Price: ₹1,000
Early turnaround + reasonable growth outlook

**Bajaj Housing Finance Ltd**
Underweight | Target Price: ₹70
Premium valuation appears **unsustainable**

**LIC Housing Finance Ltd**
Neutral | Target Price: ₹580
Market share pressure, but **valuations limit downside**

**Impact:** 🟡 Mixed — growth moderating, selective opportunities remain

**CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions Pvt Ltd – HSBC View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹1,150

India’s largest **renewable energy provider for C&I segment**
Growth driven by **increasing green energy adoption**
Renewables are **30–45% cheaper than grid power**
EBITDA expected to grow at **~60% CAGR (FY26–28)**

Impact: Positive

**ACME Solar Holdings Ltd – HSBC View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹350

One of India’s **fastest-growing vertically integrated renewable IPPs**
~6 GW contracted capacity provides **strong earnings visibility**
FDRE projects & BESS to **enhance returns profile**
EBITDA expected to grow at **~72% CAGR (FY26–28)**

Impact: Positive

**OIL & GAS – Nomura View:**

War risk premium returning as **peace talks stall**
Risk of **Strait of Hormuz disruption** if US enforces blockade
Full blockade could impact **~2.3 mbpd oil supply**
Potential disruption to **India’s LPG supplies** despite recent tanker flows

**Impact:** 🔴 Negative — supply risk & volatility in crude prices

**Ujjivan Small Finance Bank – Axis Capital View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹77 (earlier ₹66)

Transitioning towards a **diversified universal banking model**
Secured mix improving (~49%), targeting **65–70% by FY30**
Strong growth: **loans ↑ 26% YoY; disbursements at record highs**
Asset quality normalised; **collections ~99.8%**
CASA expected to **improve gradually**
Capital raise likely to **support next growth phase**

Impact: Positive

**Eternal Ltd – Goldman Sachs View:**

Recommendation: Buy
Target Price: ₹350 (earlier ₹380)

Total addressable market concerns for Blinkit may be overdone
Blinkit expected to continue gaining share vs Swiggy
Multiple levers available to offset potential headwinds
Favorable risk-reward: ~74% upside vs ~18% downside

Impact: Positive

**Dixon Technologies (India) Ltd – HSBC View:**

Recommendation: Hold
Target Price: ₹11,500

Vivo JV approval delay to impact FY27 volumes
FY27 estimates cut by ~8% (top-line impact)

**Tata Sons: The Listing Dilemma**

The RBI’s draft guidelines (April 2026) are a game-changer for India's massive shadow banks. By swapping a complex "scoring" model for a flat ₹1 lakh crore asset threshold, the regulator has effectively boxed in large conglomerates. For Tata Sons, with assets near ₹1.75 lakh crore, the "Upper Layer" (NBFC-UL) tag is now definitive, stripping away the ability to lower debt scores to avoid regulation.

The catch? While the classification is clear, the mandatory listing requirement remains a gray area for Core Investment Companies (CICs). After missing the September 2025 deadline, Tata Sons is in a high-stakes standoff: either convince the RBI to accept its registration surrender or bow to mounting pressure from the SP Group and list. If the draft becomes law, the "extraordinary" escape routes are closing fast.

India’s money used to be wild

Phooti kaudi to rupee, what a journey 🔥
